meta.title: Q3 2026 Business Performance Report
meta.lead: A detailed view of the quarter — revenue against target, the drivers behind the beat, client health, delivery capacity, and the decisions requested for Q4. Read top-to-bottom, jump via the contents panel, or print to PDF.
meta.author: Finance & Operations

sections.heading: Executive summary
paragraphs: The business closed Q3 at $4.82m revenue, 4% above the committed plan and 9% up on Q2. The beat was driven by two multi-year enterprise renewals and the on-time phase-1 delivery of the logistics engagement; it was partially offset by two new-client starts slipping into Q4.

Margin improved 1.2 points to 63.4% on better delivery utilisation. The one deteriorating indicator is NPS, down 3 points and concentrated in the mid-market segment — a recovery plan is funded and underway.
Three decisions are requested for Q4: hiring ahead of demand, funding the client-success recovery plan, and committing resources to the public-sector bid through its November decision date.

sections.heading: Revenue vs target
sections.note: Monthly revenue against the January plan, $m.
sections.body: July closed marginally under plan; August and September both beat target. The exit run-rate entering Q4 is $1.73m/month, giving comfortable coverage of the Q4 committed number even before the slipped starts land.

cards.heading: Enterprise renewals
cards.text: Both flagship accounts renewed multi-year with expanded scope.

cards.heading: Logistics go-live
cards.text: Phase 1 delivered on time; phase 2 booked for Q4.

cards.heading: Advisory upsell
cards.text: Optimisation reviews converted into three delivery engagements.

cards.heading: Drag: onboarding lag
cards.text: Two new-client starts slipped into Q4, deferring recognised revenue.

sections.heading: Pipeline and conversion
sections.note: Coverage improved; the conversion cycle lengthened.

items: Two strategic pursuits at contract stage
